item.title: BlackRock’s Tseng to Exit as CEO of Troubled Private Credit Fund
item.description: The head of BlackRock Inc.’s beleaguered private credit fund is in the process of leaving the firm, a move that follows months of losses on soured loans and revelations of a US regulatory probe into the unit’s valuation practices.

item.title: Russian Gasoline Prices Rise Again After Ukraine Hits Refineries
item.description: Retail fuel prices in Russia notched up another significant increase as recent Ukrainian drone strikes on the country’s oil refineries have led to a nationwide gasoline shortage, long lines at pumping stations and renewed concerns about accelerating inflation.

item.title: Hungarian Mayor Tells Battery Maker to Leave Over Pollution Case
item.description: The mayor of Hungary’s second-largest city called on Chinese battery-parts maker Semcorp to walk away from a new, 65 billion-forint ($210 million) factory that’s been closed for alleged environmental violations.

item.title: Slovakia to Revive Aluminum Plant That Was Once a Top Producer
item.description: Slovakia is set to revive primary aluminum production at a plant that was once one of Europe’s biggest producers of the metal.

item.title: AOL Owner Bending Spoons Jumps 14% After $1.68 Billion IPO
item.description: Bending Spoons SpA shares rose 14% above their initial public offering price after the company and some of its backers raised $1.68 billion.

item.title: Trump’s $1.4 Billion Haul Makes Him Biggest US Crypto Moneymaker
item.description: Donald Trump, who reversed his early skepticism of crypto to become one of the industry’s biggest boosters, reported more crypto-related income last year than any publicly traded US digital-asset company earned.

item.title: Warsh Signals Long Road Ahead for a Smaller Fed Balance Sheet
item.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh reiterated his preference for the US central bank to scale back its bond portfolio, while highlighting that any such step will only be made after extensive public preparation.

item.title: Brightline Hurtles Toward Debt Deadline With No Plan in Place
item.description: It’s a date that lenders to Brightline, the debt-laden private Florida railroad, have had circled for months: July 1. That’s when the company will likely look to scrounge up what’s left in reserves to pay bondholders and stave off a formal default for the last time.

item.title: UAE Oil Flows Hit Pre-War Levels on Dark Ships and Hormuz Bypass
item.description: The United United Arab Emirates has restored its oil exports to pre-war levels by sneaking tankers through the Strait of Hormuz undetected and using a pipeline that bypasses the waterway.

item.title: BlackRock Says Past Defaults, Next Election Cap Argentina Rally
item.description: Argentina’s stellar bond rally is set to hit a wall as next year’s presidential elections revive concern over the country’s turbulent economic past, according to Pablo Goldberg, an emerging markets portfolio manager at BlackRock Inc.

item.title: Electric Bike Rental Firm Lime Rises 8% After $174 Million IPO
item.description: The operator of Lime electric scooters and bikes gained 8% after the company and some of its shareholders raised $174 million in an initial public offering.

item.title: Colombia’s Incoming Finance Minister Pledges Fiscal Austerity
item.description: Colombia’s incoming finance minister is pledging to implement tighter fiscal policy, warning that the nation’s budget deficit is significantly wider than official figures suggest.

item.title: One Almost-Goldilocks Report
item.description: While we wait for the jobs data.

item.title: US Decides Against Renewing USMCA, Shifting to Rolling Talks
item.description: The US decided against renewing its trade deal with Canada and Mexico, US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said, choosing instead to conduct annual reviews of the pact in a move that risks adding uncertainty for companies producing goods across North America.

item.title: WhatsApp’s New Feature Has Defenses Against Scams, Meta Says
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. said Wednesday it’ll allow high-profile names to be only claimed by legitimate owners as part of its effort to counter impersonation on WhatsApp’s new username feature.

item.title: Warsh Taps Ex-BOE Chief King for New Fed Communications Task Force
item.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh has tapped former Bank of England Governor Mervyn King to co-chair the US central bank’s new task force on communications.

item.title: US Aluminum Smelter Idled Since 2024 to Restart as Trump Looks to Revive Industry
item.description: An idled aluminum smelter in Missouri will restart production before the end of this year, helping to boost output of the metal amid the Trump administration’s push to revive the domestic industry.

item.title: ECB’s Kaasik Says One More Rate Hike Is Reasonable Expectation
item.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Ulo Kaasik said further moentary tightening may be needed to ensure inflation returns to target after the Iran war.

item.title: Krafton Agrees to Pay ‘Subnautica 2’ Bonuses as Developer’s CEO Resigns
item.description: After being fired over a $250 million payout dispute and then reinstated, Unknown Worlds Entertainment boss Ted Gill is leaving again

item.title: Bank of England’s Bailey Says Rate Cuts Are Still Off the Table
item.description: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey cautioned that it is too early to consider interest-rate cuts, warning households are yet to feel the full effect of the Iran war.

item.title: Peru’s Inflation Unexpectedly Picks Up With Rising Food Prices
item.description: Inflation in Peru’s capital unexpectedly accelerated in June, staying above the central bank’s target range for a fourth straight month as rising food costs add to the recent global energy shock.

item.title: Uber Shakes Up AI Data Labeling Business, Dismissing Top Leaders
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. has dismissed two tech leaders at its nascent AI data labeling business, shaking up a key division that the ride-hail company has positioned as a key growth driver.

item.title: Pimco Warns Private Credit ‘Confidence Gap’ to Reveal Weak Funds
item.description: Private credit investors have become increasingly discerning about dispersion in investment marks, creating a “confidence gap” between asset managers, according to Pacific Investment Management Co.

item.title: US Manufacturing Expands at Slower Pace, Costs Gauge Drops
item.description: US manufacturing activity expanded for a sixth straight month in June as a war-driven surge in input costs eased.

item.title: Milei Tries Again to Revive Argentina’s Dormant Mortgage Market
item.description: President Javier Milei is trying to tackle Argentina’s longstanding lack of home mortgages in a bid to turn around a construction sector that’s losing jobs before his reelection bid next year.

item.title: Lagarde Says Inflation, Growth Risks Now More Broadly Balanced
item.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said risks to euro-area inflation and growth have become less pronounced.

item.title: Warsh Says Inflation Risks Are Down, Vows Price Stability
item.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh said price risks have come down in recent weeks, while repeating his determination to bring inflation back to the US central bank’s 2% target.

item.title: Sony to End Production of Physical PlayStation Discs in 2028
item.description: Sony Group Corp. will end production of physical discs for its PlayStation video-game consoles starting in January 2028, going all in on a digital approach that will require consumers to download titles from the company’s online storefront moving forward.

item.title: US Limits on Anthropic’s Mythos Keep Foreign Firms in Limbo
item.description: Foreign governments, companies and financial institutions were left in limbo after the Trump administration continued to restrict access to Anthropic PBC’s Mythos 5 to a limited number of US organizations even after dropping export controls on the company’s latest artificial intelligence models.

item.title: US Stocks Fall as Traders Evaluate Warsh Comments, New Data
item.description: US stocks pared earlier losses as fresh economic data painted a picture of resilience, while traders assessed commentary from Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh.
